MASK_LOCKED_OPTIMIZATIONS.title: Unique Optimizations Only Possible with Mask-Locked Weights
MASK_LOCKED_OPTIMIZATIONS.subtitle: Technical Analysis for BitNet b1.58-2B-4T Hardware Implementation

executive_summary.core_thesis: Mask-locked weights (etched in metal layers) create optimization opportunities that are IMPOSSIBLE for software-programmable inference. These optimizations derive from the fundamental constraint that weights are immutable at runtime.
key_findings: Precomputation of weight-dependent values eliminates 40-60% of runtime computation
Silicon-specific optimizations enable 10-100x energy reduction vs SRAM-based weights
Deterministic data flow enables hardwired routing with zero arbitration overhead
Fixed weights enable exact error characterization and precomputed correction
Ternary weights enable novel analog computation approaches
executive_summary.estimated_efficiency_gain: 10-100x energy efficiency improvement over SRAM-based inference

precomputation.title: Precomputation Opportunities
precomputation.principle: If weights W are fixed, any function f(W) that depends ONLY on weights can be precomputed at fabrication time

matrix_decompositions.description: Decompose weight matrices offline for optimized computation

techniques.name: SVD Precomputation
techniques.applicable: For any fixed weight matrix W
techniques.runtime_benefit: Replace matrix multiply with three smaller operations
techniques.memory_cost: Store U, Σ, V matrices (may increase storage)
techniques.complexity_reduction: O(n³) → O(k × n²) for rank-k approximation
techniques.applicability_to_ternary: Limited - ternary weights sparse, SVD may not help

techniques.name: QR Decomposition
techniques.offline_compute: W = QR where Q is orthogonal, R is upper triangular
techniques.applicability_to_ternary: Moderate - useful for attention Q/K projections

techniques.name: Ternary-Specific Sparse Pattern Encoding
techniques.offline_compute: Identify all non-zero positions in ternary weight matrices
techniques.runtime_benefit: Only compute MAC for non-zero weights (skip zeros)

normalization_parameters.description: Precompute statistics that depend on weight distributions

techniques.name: Weight-Dependent LayerNorm Scales
techniques.insight: LayerNorm depends on activation statistics, but weight distribution affects expected activation distribution
techniques.offline_compute: Expected mean/variance of layer outputs given typical input distributions
techniques.runtime_benefit: Initialize LayerNorm with optimal values, reduce warmup

techniques.name: Quantization Scale Factors
techniques.insight: For ternary weights, the scale factor is determined during training
techniques.offline_compute: Per-layer or per-channel scale factors are FIXED
techniques.applicability: CRITICAL - BitNet uses learned scale factors that are part of the model

techniques.name: Precomputed Attention Patterns
techniques.insight: For some attention heads, the pattern of important tokens can be pre-analyzed
techniques.offline_compute: Statistical analysis of attention head behavior on representative inputs
techniques.applicability: Limited - attention patterns depend on input

techniques.name: Fixed Q/K Projection Fusion
techniques.insight: Q and K projections use fixed weight matrices
techniques.offline_compute: Fuse Q and K computation into single operation
techniques.applicability: HIGH - weights are fixed, can optimize data flow

silicon_specific.title: Silicon-Specific Optimizations
silicon_specific.principle: Weights in metal layers have fundamentally different properties than SRAM/DRAM stored weights

metal_layer_encoding.description: Encode ternary weights directly in metal interconnect patterns

techniques.name: Via Pattern Encoding

encoding_scheme.weight_minus_one: Via present with inverted signal path

techniques.area_efficiency: Much higher than SRAM (no 6T cell per weight)
techniques.access_energy: ~0.01-0.1 pJ per access (vs ~10 pJ for SRAM)
techniques.notes: Via programming is part of metal mask, determined at fab time

three_d_integration.description: Use 3D stacking to separate weight storage from compute

approaches.name: Weight Layer on Top Metal
approaches.configuration: Weights in M8-M10 layers, compute in lower layers
approaches.advantage: Minimal routing distance to MAC units
approaches.heat_dissipation: Good - compute heat doesn't affect weights
approaches.applicability: Standard CMOS process, no exotic stacking needed

approaches.name: Bit-Position Layering
approaches.insight: For quantized weights, different bit positions can be on different layers
approaches.configuration: MSB layer closer to compute, LSB layer farther
approaches.advantage: Enable early-termination computation (stop if MSB zero)
approaches.applicability: More relevant for 4+ bit weights, not ternary

vs_sram.area: Mask ROM: ~0.05 μm²/bit vs SRAM: ~0.84 μm²/bit (16x denser)
vs_sram.power: Mask ROM: ~0 (static) vs SRAM: leakage + refresh power
vs_sram.access_time: Mask ROM: ~1ns vs SRAM: ~0.5ns (combinational vs sequential)
vs_sram.flexibility: Mask ROM: None vs SRAM: Full reprogrammability

ternary_specific.area_per_trit: Can encode ternary in single via presence/absence + polarity
ternary_specific.estimated_density: 2-3x denser than binary mask ROM equivalent
ternary_specific.notes: Ternary enables more information per physical structure

hardwired_broadcast.description: Broadcast weight values through metal traces to multiple MAC units

technique.name: Weight Broadcasting Tree
technique.implementation: Single weight value drives multiple MAC units simultaneously
technique.advantage: One weight read serves N computations
technique.efficiency: N-way reduction in weight access energy

routing.title: Routing Optimizations for Deterministic Data Flow
routing.principle: With fixed weights and fixed model architecture, data flow is 100% deterministic

techniques.name: Fixed Layer-to-Layer Connections
techniques.insight: In a fixed model, the output of layer N always goes to layer N+1
techniques.implementation: Dedicated metal traces between layer outputs and inputs
techniques.advantage: Zero routing overhead, no network-on-chip needed

techniques.name: Attention Head Routing
techniques.insight: Number and configuration of attention heads is fixed
techniques.implementation: Hardwired connections from each head to output aggregation
techniques.advantage: Parallel head computation with deterministic merge

systolic_array_optimization.description: Weight-stationary systolic array with permanently-stored weights

configuration.architecture: 2D systolic array with weights locked in each processing element
configuration.weight_storage: No storage needed - weights are the PE configuration
configuration.data_flow: Activations flow through array, weights stay fixed
configuration.peak_efficiency: 100% PE utilization possible (no weight load overhead)

comparison.throughput_improvement: 1.1-1.4x same clock, but lower power enables higher clock

direct_connect_mac.description: Connect MAC units directly without intermediate buffering

technique.name: Pipeline-Direct MAC Chaining
technique.implementation: Output of MAC[i] directly feeds MAC[i+1] for accumulation

error_correction.title: Error Analysis and Correction for Fixed Weights
error_correction.principle: With fixed weights, all errors can be characterized exactly during design/fab

quantization_error_characterization.description: Ternary quantization error is deterministic for each weight

offline_analysis.name: Per-Weight Error Table
offline_analysis.compute: For each weight: error = original_fp32 - quantized_ternary × scale
offline_analysis.storage: Error table stored alongside weights (or precompensated)
offline_analysis.feasibility: Large storage overhead - better to incorporate into training

offline_analysis.name: Statistical Error Characterization
offline_analysis.compute: Distribution of errors across each layer

process_variation_compensation.description: Manufacturing variations create systematic errors in weight encoding

techniques.name: Post-Fab Calibration
techniques.method: Test each chip with known inputs, measure outputs
techniques.runtime_cost: Minimal - just apply correction to outputs

deterministic_error_bounds.description: For mask-locked weights, error bounds are FIXED and knowable
advantages: Can prove worst-case error bounds mathematically
Safety-critical applications can rely on guaranteed precision

comparison_to_runtime_quantization.runtime: Error varies with input distribution, dynamic range
comparison_to_runtime_quantization.mask_locked: Error is static, can be fully characterized once

precomputed_lut_correction.description: For certain operations, use precomputed lookup tables for error correction

technique.name: Output Correction LUT
technique.method: Precompute expected error for each possible output range
technique.runtime: Single LUT lookup per layer output

exact_dimensions.note: Based on BitNet b1.58-2B-4T technical report (arXiv:2504.12285)

weights_breakdown.attention_per_layer: 4 × hidden_dim × hidden_dim (Q, K, V, O projections)

layer_specific_optimizations.mask_locked_optimization: Direct metal routing from token index to embedding vector
layer_specific_optimizations.recommendation: Store embedding in mask ROM, use sparse access patterns
Fused Q/K/V projection unit with zero-skipping

layer_specific_optimizations.mask_locked_optimization: Direct routing from attention heads to output

layer_specific_optimizations.recommendation: Sparse compute, early termination for zeros

per_token_operations.total_macs_per_token: ~8-12B MACs per token generated (estimated for 2B model)

with_ternary_optimization.mac_to_add: Ternary MAC becomes: conditional add/sub (no multiplication)
with_ternary_optimization.operation_reduction: ~3x fewer gate operations than INT8 multiply

per_layer_optimization_table.description: Each layer can be individually optimized since dimensions are fixed
optimizations: Hardwire exact matrix dimensions for each layer
Custom MAC array size per layer (no generic array needed)
Pipeline depth matched to layer computation
Register allocation optimized for fixed intermediate sizes

analog_opportunities.title: Analog Computing Opportunities for Ternary Weights
analog_opportunities.principle: Ternary values {-1, 0, +1} map naturally to analog voltage levels

voltage_encoding.description: Encode ternary weights as discrete voltage levels

encoding_schemes.name: Symmetric Voltage Encoding
encoding_schemes.challenge: Requires negative voltage rails or differential signaling

encoding_schemes.name: Single-Ended Encoding
encoding_schemes.challenge: Zero state requires reference, more complex comparison

analog_mac.description: Implement MAC operations in analog domain

approaches.name: Current-Mode Accumulation
approaches.principle: Weight voltage × activation voltage = current; sum currents
approaches.implementation: Transconductance amplifiers for V-to-I, current summing node
approaches.energy: ~0.1-1 pJ per MAC (100x better than digital)
approaches.speed: Limited by RC time constants, ~10-100 MHz
approaches.challenge: Process variation, noise, need ADC for output

approaches.name: Charge-Domain Computation
approaches.principle: Weight controls capacitor switching; sum charges

approaches.name: Memristor Crossbar
approaches.principle: Weight stored as memristor conductance; activation as voltage; output is current
approaches.challenge_for_ternary: Memristors typically store 2+ bits, overkill for ternary
approaches.ternary_adaptation: Use 1-bit memristor (binary) + sign bit from separate device
approaches.accuracy: Limited by device variation, typically 4-6 bits

mixed_signal_architecture.description: Hybrid analog-digital architecture for optimal efficiency

vs_binary_analog.information_density: log2(3) = 1.58 bits per element vs 1 bit
vs_binary_analog.dynamic_range: Built-in zero state for sparse computation
vs_binary_analog.natural_representation: Three states match many physical systems

vs_multilevel_analog.robustness: Only 3 levels vs 4+ levels, larger noise margin
vs_multilevel_analog.process_tolerance: Easier to distinguish 3 levels than 16+ levels

emerging_technologies.name: Domain-Wall Motion Devices
emerging_technologies.ternary_fit: Could encode ternary in magnetic domain position

unique_advantage_summary.title: Optimizations ONLY Possible with Mask-Locked Weights

impossible_for_programmable.description: Weights are hardwired, no memory read cycle
impossible_for_programmable.programmable_limitation: Must always fetch weights from SRAM/DRAM

impossible_for_programmable.description: No dynamic power for weight storage
impossible_for_programmable.programmable_limitation: SRAM leakage + read power always consumed

impossible_for_programmable.description: Can prove error bounds mathematically before fabrication
impossible_for_programmable.programmable_limitation: Errors vary with which model is loaded

impossible_for_programmable.description: No routing logic, arbitration, or buffers needed
Weights can be voltage levels, not digital values
Each chip can be calibrated to its specific process corner
impossible_for_programmable.programmable_limitation: Cannot optimize for unknown future models

impossible_for_programmable.description: Zeros are literally 'no connection' - zero silicon area
impossible_for_programmable.programmable_limitation: Sparse storage still requires index bits

impossible_for_programmable.description: Weights can occupy top metal layers, compute below
impossible_for_programmable.programmable_limitation: Reprogramming requires transistor access to every weight

latency.deterministic: Exact latency known at design time

tradeoffs.flexibility: ZERO - can never run a different model
tradeoffs.upgrade_path: New chip required for model updates
tradeoffs.development_cost: Higher NRE for each model variant
tradeoffs.risk: Model obsolescence could render chips useless

phase_1_digital.approach: Pure digital implementation with via-encoded weights

phase_2_mixed_signal.approach: Mixed-signal with analog MAC and digital control

phase_3_full_analog.approach: Full analog weight storage and computation
phase_3_full_analog.timeline: 36-48 months, may require new device technology

references.ternary_hardware: Design implementations of ternary logic systems (ScienceDirect, 2024)
references.analog_neural: Ternary Neural Networks Based on on/off Memristors (2022)
